There is a snake (a one-cell wide path) in the grid that may touch itself diagonally but may not touch itself orthogonally. The head and tail of the snake are given by circles. Cells in the grid are either on the snake or in one of several non-snake regions (groups of orthogonally connected cells that are bordered by snake cells and/or the edges of the grid). A digit in a square gives the number of cells in that square\'s non-snake region. Digits on the snake are subject to the following constraints: Within each 3x3 box, adjacent digits on the snake must have a difference of at least 5. Across the bold border between two 3x3 boxes, adjacent digits on the snake must have a difference of at least 4.'},{v:'solution: 463728915829531647175496283917385426248967531356142798782619354531274869694853172'}],o:[{ct:[7.5,2.5],w:0.75,h:0.75,c1:#0,c2:#F,r:f,fs:12,te:''},{ct:[8.5,5.5],w:0.75,h:0.75,c1:#0,c2:#F,r:f,fs:12,te:''},{ct:[4.5,2.5],w:0.75,h:0.75,c1:#0,c2:#F,r:f,fs:12,te:''},{ct:[2.5,6.5],w:0.75,h:0.75,c1:#0,c2:#F,r:t,fs:12,te:''},{ct:[3.5,8.5],w:0.75,h:0.75,c1:#0,c2:#F,r:t,fs:12,te:''}],vd:[{id:'g_kUaaYFGJ0',publishedAt:'2023-08-03T22:00:20Z',t:'Dumb and Dumber ... or Hard and Harder?',d:0}]}
